evals.prompt: Give me a weekly options trade plan for AAPL.
evals.expected_output: A self-contained HTML file named trade_plan_AAPL_<expiry>.html. Expiry resolves to the nearest Friday (0–7 DTE). Short strikes target ~0.15 delta and $5-wide spreads. Defined-risk only (no naked strangle). Management cutoff is Thursday if short strike is threatened. Output contains: masthead, data band with IV/HV/ratio, executive summary, three color-coded trade cards (bull put, iron condor, bear call), decision flowchart with real S1/R1 levels, Chart.js P&L overlay of all three strategies, sizing table at $250k/$500k/$1M, data sources with timestamps.

assertions.name: fetch_script_executed
assertions.description: The skill ran fetch_trade_plan_data.py via Bash before generating output (visible in tool calls/transcript).

assertions.name: weekly_expiry_resolved
assertions.description: Expiry date in the output is a Friday within the next 7 days and DTE is <= 7.

assertions.name: fifteen_delta_shorts
assertions.description: Both the bull put short and bear call short strike deltas are in the 0.12–0.18 range.

assertions.name: five_wide_spreads
assertions.description: Spread widths are $5 for both the bull put and bear call legs.

assertions.name: thursday_management_rule
assertions.description: Management timeline includes a Thursday cutoff rule for the weekly DTE bucket.

assertions.name: html_output_produced
assertions.description: A complete .html file is written to disk — includes <!doctype html>, Chart.js, and all required sections.

assertions.name: three_color_coded_cards
assertions.description: Output contains three trade cards using the bull (green), neutral (blue), and bear (red) colour scheme.

assertions.name: pnl_chart_present
assertions.description: HTML contains a Chart.js canvas with three datasets (bullPut, condor, bearCall).

assertions.name: sizing_table_three_sizes
assertions.description: Sizing table shows at least $250k, $500k, and $1M columns.

assertions.name: premiums_labelled_model_estimate
assertions.description: Option premiums are labelled as model estimates (Black-Scholes from mid), not live quotes.

evals.expected_output: HTML plan resolving to the nearest listed expiry at or beyond 45 DTE. Short strikes target 0.20–0.25 delta, $10-wide spreads. 21-DTE exit rule in management timeline. Iron condor gets the primary recommendation given the explicit user ask; bull put and bear call still presented as Scenario A/C. Must detect if TSLA earnings fall in window and flag them.

assertions.name: dte_near_45
assertions.description: Resolved DTE is between 35 and 75 days (nearest standard expiry at or beyond 45 DTE).

assertions.name: twenty_to_25_delta
assertions.description: Short strikes for both sides target 0.18–0.28 delta.

assertions.name: ten_wide_spreads
assertions.description: Spread widths are ~$10 on both sides of the condor.

assertions.name: twenty_one_dte_exit
assertions.description: Management timeline explicitly calls for 21-DTE exit rule (or 50% profit target, whichever first).

assertions.name: iron_condor_primary
assertions.description: The iron condor is presented as the primary recommendation (matches the user's explicit request).

assertions.name: earnings_handled
assertions.description: If TSLA earnings fall within the expiry window, they are flagged in the executive summary and handled (widen shorts OR alternative strategy). If not in window, the earnings timing is still noted.

assertions.name: html_output_produced
assertions.description: Complete HTML file written to disk with required sections.

evals.prompt: SPY monthly spread strategy for May expiry.
evals.expected_output: HTML plan resolving to the 3rd Friday of May. DTE calculated. Since SPY is an index ETF, no single-company earnings — but component-level macro catalysts (CPI, FOMC, jobs reports) should be checked and mentioned. Wider strikes given DTE 22–45 bucket. Output is the full editorial HTML document.

assertions.name: may_third_friday
assertions.description: Expiry is the 3rd Friday of May (or the nearest listed standard expiry).

assertions.name: monthly_delta_bucket
assertions.description: Short strikes target ~0.20–0.25 delta (monthly DTE bucket).

assertions.name: macro_catalysts_mentioned
assertions.description: Market context section notes macro catalysts (CPI, FOMC, jobs, or similar) relevant to SPY rather than ticker-specific earnings.

assertions.name: html_output_produced
assertions.description: Complete HTML file written to disk with required sections.

assertions.name: expected_move_calculated
assertions.description: Expected move stated as ±$ and ±%, derived from price × IV × sqrt(DTE/365).

evals.prompt: NVDA options trade plan, 30 days out, I have $500k.
evals.expected_output: HTML plan resolving to ~30 DTE. Sizing table anchored to $500k (plus $250k and $1M for comparison). Checks if NVDA earnings fall within the window — NVDA reports quarterly, so this is a live possibility. Monthly bucket defaults (0.20–0.25 delta, $10 wide).

assertions.name: dte_near_30
assertions.description: Resolved DTE is 21–45 (nearest listed expiry at or beyond 30 DTE).

assertions.name: sizing_anchored_500k
assertions.description: $500k is prominent in the sizing table (either highlighted or placed first).

assertions.name: nvda_earnings_checked
assertions.description: Earnings section explicitly addresses NVDA earnings timing relative to expiry (in-window, near-window, or clearly outside).

assertions.name: html_output_produced
assertions.description: Complete HTML file written to disk with required sections.

assertions.name: contracts_calculated
assertions.description: Sizing table shows a contract count for each strategy at each portfolio size, computed as floor((portfolio × budget%) / max_loss_per_lot).

evals.prompt: What spread should I sell on MSFT this week?
evals.expected_output: Weekly expiry (nearest Friday). Searches for MSFT earnings and near-term catalysts. Full HTML plan produced. No directional bias given — neutral default → iron condor primary; bull put and bear call as the bullish/bearish alternates.

assertions.name: weekly_expiry_resolved
assertions.description: Expiry is the nearest Friday, DTE <= 7.

assertions.name: neutral_default
assertions.description: Because no bias was given, the iron condor is presented as the primary recommendation under the neutral assumption (explicitly stated).

assertions.name: msft_earnings_checked
assertions.description: Earnings section addresses MSFT earnings timing.

assertions.name: catalyst_search_performed
assertions.description: Market context includes recent MSFT news/catalyst (visible in transcript as web search).

assertions.name: html_output_produced
assertions.description: Complete HTML file written to disk with required sections.

evals.prompt: AMZN, I'm slightly bearish, 3 weeks out.
evals.expected_output: Expiry ~21 DTE. Bear call spread is the primary recommendation given the slight bearish bias. Bull put and iron condor presented as alternates. Entry trigger for the bear call is tied to a resistance level rejection (pivot R1, call-OI wall, or SMA). Bi-weekly bucket: ~0.18 delta, $5–$10 width.

assertions.name: dte_near_21
assertions.description: Resolved DTE is 14–28 days.

assertions.name: bear_call_primary
assertions.description: Bear call spread is highlighted as the primary recommendation for the stated slight-bearish bias.

assertions.name: resistance_rejection_entry
assertions.description: Entry trigger for the bear call references a specific resistance level (pivot R1, call-OI wall, or SMA) and a rejection/failed-breakout pattern — not a generic 'enter if bearish'.

assertions.name: eighteen_delta_shorts
assertions.description: Short strikes target ~0.15–0.22 delta (bi-weekly bucket).

assertions.name: html_output_produced
assertions.description: Complete HTML file written to disk with required sections.

evals.expected_output: ~60 DTE expiry. Strikes at 0.25–0.30 delta, wider widths (~$10–$15). Extended DTE bucket — the plan mentions diagonals/calendars as alternatives. Checks for macro events (FOMC, CPI, jobs, elections) in the 60-day window. Management timeline: 50% profit target or close at 30 DTE remaining.

assertions.name: dte_near_60
assertions.description: Resolved DTE is 46–90 days.

assertions.name: extended_delta_bucket
assertions.description: Short strikes target ~0.23–0.32 delta (extended bucket).

assertions.name: wider_spreads
assertions.description: Spread widths are $10 or wider.

assertions.name: diagonal_calendar_mentioned
assertions.description: Plan explicitly mentions diagonals or calendars as alternatives for the extended DTE.

assertions.name: thirty_dte_exit_rule
assertions.description: Management timeline references closing at 30 DTE remaining (or 50% profit target).

assertions.name: macro_window_checked
assertions.description: Market context or earnings section notes macro events in the 60-day window.

assertions.name: html_output_produced
assertions.description: Complete HTML file written to disk with required sections.

evals.prompt: Give me a 90 day options strategy for GOOGL.
evals.expected_output: DTE ~90 or slightly above. Plan flags that standard premium-selling weeklies/monthlies are suboptimal at this DTE and recommends a diagonal or calendar, explaining theta decay rationale. Still produces the full editorial HTML with useful spread analysis for context.

assertions.name: leaps_flag_explicit
assertions.description: Plan explicitly warns that standard short-premium strategies are suboptimal at >90 DTE and recommends a diagonal or calendar.

assertions.name: theta_rationale_given
assertions.description: Plan explains why: slow theta decay / unfavorable risk-reward at extended DTE.

assertions.name: full_html_still_produced
assertions.description: Complete HTML file is still produced — the skill does not refuse, but clearly labels spreads as suboptimal for this DTE.

assertions.name: googl_earnings_checked
assertions.description: Earnings section addresses GOOGL earnings timing relative to expiry.

evals.expected_output: Weekly expiry. Earnings flagged prominently in the Executive Summary because they fall within the expiry window. Either widens short strikes by 1.5× earnings expected move OR recommends against outright premium selling and suggests alternatives (long strangle, calendar, iron fly). Cannot silently ignore earnings.

assertions.name: earnings_flagged_exec_summary
assertions.description: Executive summary prominently flags earnings within the expiry window.

assertions.name: earnings_handling_explicit
assertions.description: Plan makes an explicit choice: widen short strikes by 1.5× earnings EM, OR recommend avoiding premium selling and suggest an alternative (long strangle, calendar, iron fly). States which and why.

assertions.name: not_silently_ignored
assertions.description: Earnings are not mentioned only as a passing note — they drive a structural decision in the plan.

assertions.name: html_output_produced
assertions.description: Complete HTML file written to disk with required sections.

evals.expected_output: No timeframe specified — defaults to the nearest weekly Friday and explicitly states this assumption at the top. Full HTML plan, weekly bucket defaults, NVDA earnings checked.

assertions.name: default_weekly_stated
assertions.description: Plan explicitly states that no timeframe was given and the default (nearest weekly Friday) is being used.

assertions.name: weekly_expiry_resolved
assertions.description: Expiry is the nearest Friday, DTE <= 7.

assertions.name: nvda_earnings_checked
assertions.description: Earnings section addresses NVDA earnings timing.

assertions.name: html_output_produced
assertions.description: Complete HTML file written to disk with required sections.
